findings.justification: Profile links and user status strings are concatenated into $banned_profile_links without HTML escaping before being substituted into the admin email template, so a user with a maliciously crafted display name or profile URL can inject arbitrary HTML into administrator notification emails.

$_REQUEST is used instead of $_POST when checking the nonce and action, which merges GET, POST, and COOKIE inputs and can allow an attacker to supply the nonce via a query-string parameter or cookie rather than a form POST body, weakening the intended anti-CSRF protection.
